title: Randia matudae
taxonomy: Randia matudae belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family comprises over 13,000 species of flowering plants. The genus Randia is a part of this family and consists of tropical trees and shrubs found in Central and South America, as well as the Caribbean. As a member of the genus Randia, Randia matudae is a flowering plant with specific characteristics and growth habits. Further classification of Randia matudae may also include its order, class, and kingdom within the plant taxonomy.
uses: Randia matudae is reported to have various medicinal uses, although the information is limited. intro: Randia matudae, also known as the Matud's Randia, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in the tropical forests of the Philippines. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Randia matudae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and also used to make herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures to treat various health conditions.
